title: coelachne angolensis
intro: Coelachne angolensis, also known as Angolan grass or snake grass, is a type of grass native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, this plant has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Coelachne angolensis include relief from fever, pain, and respiratory issues, as well as potential wound healing and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Coelachne angolensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions and side effects.
